title: Striatal Punch Collection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4dan","text":"This protocol describes how to collect striatal punches from fresh frozen mouse brain tissue for HPLC analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sacrifice mice with CO2, rapidly remove the brain, and freeze in superchilled isopentane on dry ice. Store brains at -80˚C until ready to collect tissue.
Embed brains in OCT, and mount on a cryostat. Trim until the striatum, then take punches from the dorsal striatum using a pipette tip cut to 1mm in diameter.
Store striatal punches at -80˚C until ready to ship for analysis. Ship on dry ice.
creator: James Maas
